explanation: This command simulates a headless conhost.exe process launching PowerShell, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria. It includes the required '--headless' and 'powershell' in the command line.
This command uses the short form of the '-Command' option as a substitution technique, maintaining the same behavior while altering the syntax slightly.
This command omits the quotes around the PowerShell command, relying on the interpreter to parse the command correctly, which is accepted in many cases.
This command adds extra whitespace at the end of the command line, which is a safe mutation technique and does not affect the execution.
This command appends a harmless pipeline to suppress output, which does not alter the detection logic but demonstrates reordering and omission techniques.
